CREATE TABLE dates_test (input_date String, output_date DateTime64(3));
INSERT INTO dates_test VALUES ('01/15/2021', NULL), ('02/29/2020', NULL), ('12/31/2023', NULL), ('04/31/2021', NULL), ('invalid date', NULL);
SELECT input_date, parseDateTime64BestEffortUSOrNull(input_date) AS output_date FROM dates_test;
CREATE TABLE nested_test AS SELECT arrayJoin(['01/15/2021', 'not a date', '02/28/2022']) AS dates;
SELECT input_date, parseDateTime64BestEffortUSOrNull(input_date) AS output_date FROM dates_test WHERE output_date IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE secondary_dates_test AS SELECT input_date, parseDateTime64BestEffortUSOrNull(input_date) AS output_date FROM dates_test;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM secondary_dates_test WHERE output_date IS NULL;
SELECT a.input_date, b.output_date FROM dates_test a JOIN secondary_dates_test b ON a.input_date = b.input_date WHERE b.output_date IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE date_ranges AS SELECT arrayJoin(range(1, 100)) AS day;
SELECT day, parseDateTime64BestEffortUSOrNull(CONCAT('01/', day, '/2021')) AS output_date FROM date_ranges;
CREATE TABLE invalid_dates_test (id Int32, invalid_date String);
SELECT id, parseDateTime64BestEffortUSOrNull(invalid_date) AS output_date FROM invalid_dates_test WHERE output_date IS NULL;
